# forecaster.metrics.calculate_coverage { #spotforecast2_safe.forecaster.metrics.calculate_coverage }

```python
forecaster.metrics.calculate_coverage(y_true, lower_bound, upper_bound)
```

Calculate coverage of a given interval.

Coverage is the proportion of true values that fall within the interval.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name        | Type                                                                                 | Description                         | Default    |
|-------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|------------|
| y_true      | [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) \| [pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`) | True values of the target variable. | _required_ |
| lower_bound | [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) \| [pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`) | Lower bound of the interval.        | _required_ |
| upper_bound | [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) \| [pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`) | Upper bound of the interval.        | _required_ |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type             | Description               |
|--------|------------------|---------------------------|
|        | [float](`float`) | Coverage of the interval. |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> from spotforecast2.forecaster.metrics import calculate_coverage
>>> y_true = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
>>> lower_bound = np.array([0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5])
>>> upper_bound = np.array([1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5])
>>> coverage = calculate_coverage(y_true, lower_bound, upper_bound)
>>> coverage == 1.0  # All values within bounds
True
```
